title: IR Ticket Stuck in Containment Beyond SLA

compliance.assurance_rationale: CSF 2.0 RS.MA-01 (Incident management) — UC tracks incident response processes and containment activities, providing partial evidence that incidents are managed.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST CSF RS.MA-01 (Incident management) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.7.39: IR Ticket Stuck in Containment Beyond SLA.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.7.39' running on snow:incident, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:incident category=security earliest=-30d state="3"
| eval hours_in_state=(now()-strptime(u_containment_start,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))/3600
| where isnotnull(u_containment_start) AND hours_in_state>8
description: Measures security incidents stuck in containment state beyond SLA for incident management effectiveness.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST CSF compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm and sourcetypes snow:incident. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: Measures security incidents stuck in containment state beyond service promises for incident management effectiveness — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
implementation: (1) Map state numbers to your instance; (2) page IR lead; (3) document blockers. Indexes required: `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=snow:incident`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `snow:incident`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Map state numbers to your instance; (2) page IR lead; (3) document blockers.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:incident category=security earliest=-30d state="3"
| eval hours_in_state=(now()-strptime(u_containment_start,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))/3600
| where isnotnull(u_containment_start) AND hours_in_state>8
```

Understanding this SPL

**IR Ticket Stuck in Containment Beyond SLA (RS.MA-01)** — Measures security incidents stuck in containment state beyond SLA for incident management effectiveness.

Documented **Data sources**: `snow:incident`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:incident. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype=snow:incident, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **hours_in_state** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(u_containment_start) AND hours_in_state>8` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Single value
knownFalsePositives: Response time may extend during shift handoffs, holiday weekends, or major incident bursts.
